prompt:
The midway roster library for our fairground season app just went under the platform team's hierarchy-hygiene flags (-Wnon-virtual-dtor -Woverloaded-virtual -Wshadow on top of the usual -Wall -Wextra -Werror) and the build went red. The ride classes themselves have been stable all season — pricing, capacity and queue waits all match the printed tariff sheet, and the tests pin those numbers — so this needs to be a cleanup, not a redesign. The dump:

    g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -Wnon-virtual-dtor -Woverloaded-virtual -Wshadow -o test_rides test_rides.cpp
    ride.h:9:7: error: 'class Ride' has virtual functions and accessible non-virtual destructor [-Werror=non-virtual-dtor]
    rides.h:6:7: error: base class 'class Ride' has accessible non-virtual destructor [-Werror=non-virtual-dtor]
    rides.h:6:7: error: 'class Coaster' has virtual functions and accessible non-virtual destructor [-Werror=non-virtual-dtor]
    ride.h:19:20: error: 'virtual double Ride::price(int) const' was hidden [-Werror=overloaded-virtual=]
    rides.h:12:12: note:   by 'double Coaster::price(int, bool) const'
    rides.h:17:13: error: declaration of 'seats' shadows a member of 'Coaster' [-Werror=shadow]
    ride.h:31:9: note: shadowed declaration is here
    rides.h:24:7: error: base class 'class Ride' has accessible non-virtual destructor [-Werror=non-virtual-dtor]
    rides.h:24:7: error: 'class Carousel' has virtual functions and accessible non-virtual destructor [-Werror=non-virtual-dtor]
    test_rides.cpp:9:18: error: no matching function for call to 'Coaster::price(int)'
    test_rides.cpp:10:18: error: no matching function for call to 'Coaster::price(int)'

That overloaded-virtual one is the same thing biting the till code, by the way — on a Coaster the till sells plain fares AND fast-lane fares, so both price forms have to keep working with their current names and signatures; the plain fare on the coaster is the standard tariff. The park roster owns rides through the base class, so mind what that first warning is protecting. Keep every number the tests pin exactly as it is, and don't touch test_rides.cpp, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5W3 item 4: -Wnon-virtual-dtor + -Woverloaded-virtual + -Wshadow sweep on a small hierarchy (Ride <- Coaster/Carousel, Park owns via unique_ptr<Ride>). Authoring toolchain: g++ 16.1.1, single-TU header-only build. Planted: (1) public non-virtual ~Ride() {} with virtuals — warning fires on all three class definitions; fix `virtual ~Ride() = default;` (no layout change: vtable already present); Park's unique_ptr<Ride> is why it matters. (2) Coaster::price(int,bool) hides virtual price(int) — probed: the warning does NOT fire if the derived class also overrides price(int), so the plant is hiding-only, with the surcharge overload calling Ride::price(age) explicitly (innocent, and keeps the buggy header self-consistent); the frozen test calls BOTH c.price(int) and c.price(int,bool), so pre-fix the dump also shows two no-matching-function errors in test_rides.cpp — deliberate: that is exactly the caller breakage -Woverloaded-virtual warns about, the prompt says both forms must keep working with current names/signatures, and the fix is `using Ride::price;` (a re-declared override that reproduces base pricing also passes — tests pin plain coaster fares AT the base tariff 2.5/4.0). (3) -Wshadow: local `int seats = capacity() - 2` in Coaster::wait_minutes shadowing member seats, same-purpose value, fix renames the local (behavior pinned by the 8-per-cycle wait tests). Behavior teeth: both price forms, virtual dispatch through const Ride& (Carousel toddler-free), capacity minus resting horses, ceil-cycle waits incl. zero-boardable edge, park totals and tie-break, empty park.
